message:
fix(daemon): suppress zram false-positive swap thrash, rate-limit scan warnings, and wire emergency escalation

Three improvements to the daemon main loop:

- Suppress false swap-thrash detection on zram-backed swap: high zram
  usage with >40% free RAM is normal compressed-memory operation, not
  disk paging. Check /sys/block/zram0 existence before flagging.

- Rate-limit scan channel saturation log messages to once per 60s.
  Previously these fired every 1-2s during sustained pressure, flooding
  logs with thousands of identical warnings.

- Force low-confidence predictive actions to Clear so they don't trigger
  downstream scan requests, breaking the scan saturation feedback loop
  where unreliable predictions cause scans that cause more predictions.

- Wire check_emergency_escalation() into the monitoring loop to break
  fallback_safe deadlock under sustained RED/Critical pressure.

- Use configurable scan_time_budget_secs from scanner config instead of
  hardcoded constant, falling back to the built-in default when 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
